reforms.description: Korea's Five-Year Plan for Green Growth includes a significant investment of 13 trillion won in R&D focused on 27 core technologies, emphasizing the importance of public R&D in addressing climate change and fostering innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in R&D for core technologies is expected to enhance innovation capabilities and contribute to economic growth through the development of new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The Five-Year Plan for Green Growth was introduced in 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a structural shift towards sustainable development and innovation in response to climate change, with significant long-term economic implications.
reforms.source_quote: The Five-Year Plan also includes 13 trillion won (1.2% of GDP) in R&D focused on 27 core technologies.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase R&D spending by 7.1% in 2010, aligning with a mid-term strategy to boost public R&D by 50% between 2008 and 2012, focusing on basic science, new growth engines, and green technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and support the development of new technologies, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to increasing R&D spending as part of its fiscal plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D, which is crucial for fostering innovation and addressing future economic challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to increase R&D spending by 7.1% in 2010, in line with the mid-term plan to boost public R&D by 50% between 2008 and 2012.

reforms.description: The Fast Track programme was launched to provide liquidity support to nearly 10,000 SMEs facing financial stress, offering measures such as new bank loans and debt restructuring.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to SMEs, the programme enhances their ability to innovate and grow, contributing positively to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme provided 18 trillion won to SMEs by mid-2009 and was extended through the end of 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant intervention to support SMEs during a financial crisis, enhancing their resilience and capacity for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Fast Track programme, which was created in October 2008, provided 18 trillion won (1.7% of GDP) to nearly 10 thousand SMEs by mid-2009 and was extended through the end of 2009.

reforms.description: The Win-Win Guarantee programme was established to facilitate support from large firms to SMEs, providing 100% guarantees on loans to recommended suppliers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This programme strengthens the supply chain by ensuring SMEs have access to necessary financing, thereby fostering innovation and growth in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme provided guarantees on loans to SMEs, with significant contributions from large corporations and banks.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a strategic collaboration between large firms and SMEs, enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The Win-Win programme is a way for large firms to support key SMEs that supply them with intermediate products.

reforms.description: Korea plans to make green growth the driver of economic development, focusing on basic R&D to develop new technologies that lower abatement costs and significantly cut greenhouse gas emissions by 2020.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in basic R&D is expected to foster innovation and technological advancements that can drive economic growth while addressing environmental challenges.
reforms.status_evidence: Korea plans to make green growth the driver of economic development.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards sustainable economic development through innovation, with potential long-term impacts on both the economy and the environment.
reforms.source_quote: Korea plans to make green growth the driver of economic development, while cutting greenhouse gas emissions significantly by 2020.

reforms.description: The government launched a major initiative in May 2009 to develop services, aiming to provide a more level playing field between services and manufacturing, which traditionally received greater support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting the development of services, the initiative aims to generate employment and boost domestic demand, which can lead to increased economic activity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was launched in May 2009 to develop services.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a structural change in how the government supports different sectors, aiming to enhance the competitiveness of the service sector, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched a major initiative in May 2009 to develop services.

reforms.description: The government has developed a strategy to enhance five service sectors, including healthcare and education, to create high-quality jobs and support economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on high-value service sectors, the strategy aims to stimulate job creation and economic growth, enhancing overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has developed a strategy to enhance service sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards service sectors, which are crucial for job creation and economic diversification.
reforms.source_quote: The government has developed a strategy to develop each of these services.

reforms.description: The Korea Fair Trade Commission identified and worked to improve 60 market entry regulations that distort market structure, aiming to enhance competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing market entry barriers, the reform is expected to foster competition and innovation, leading to improved economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The KFTC identified 60 market entry regulations and reached agreements to improve 26 of them.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses regulatory barriers that hinder competition, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Korea Fair Trade Commission identified 60 market entry regulations that distort the market structure.

reforms.description: The Korean government introduced a 100% credit guarantee scheme for firms in core sectors, including high technology and start-ups, to support their financial stability during the crisis.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme enhances access to finance for innovative firms, fostering their growth and contribution to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced the introduction of the scheme to support firms in critical sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a 100% guarantee scheme represents a significant intervention to support high-tech and start-up firms during a financial crisis, enhancing their ability to secure funding.
reforms.source_quote: A 100% guarantee scheme was introduced for firms in core sectors, including exports, green growth, high technology and start-ups.

reforms.description: Korea announced the 'National Strategy for Green Growth' in July 2009, aiming to mitigate climate change and develop new economic growth engines through targeted policies and investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy promotes the development of green technologies and industries, which can enhance productivity and create new markets.
reforms.status_evidence: The government presented the strategy and its objectives in July 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a comprehensive approach to integrating environmental sustainability with economic growth, impacting multiple sectors and long-term policy direction.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in July 2009 the 'National Strategy for Green Growth' up to 2050, which includes mitigating climate change, creating new engines for economic growth and improving the quality of life.

reforms.description: The government announced the Five-Year Plan for Green Growth, which aims to invest 2% of GDP annually from 2009 to 2013 to promote green technologies and sustainable economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan is expected to create new markets for low-carbon technologies, thereby fostering innovation and job creation in green sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was announced and is being executed with significant budget allocations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a structural shift towards a green economy, with substantial public investment aimed at fostering innovation and addressing climate change.
reforms.source_quote: The Five-Year Plan calls for spending 2% of GDP per year over the period 2009-13.

reforms.description: The government plans to expand its R&D investment in green technologies from 2 trillion won in 2009 to 3.5 trillion won by 2013, increasing the share of green R&D from 16% to 20% of total R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation in green technologies, contributing to economic growth and environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to expand its R&D investment in green technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D for green technologies, which is crucial for addressing climate change and fostering new growth engines.
reforms.source_quote: In the Five-Year Plan, the government plans to expand its R&D investment in green technologies from 2 trillion won in 2009 to 3.5 trillion won by 2013.

reforms.description: The government introduced tax incentives in 2010 for financial instruments that invest in green technology and industry, aiming to promote investment in sustainable practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Tax incentives are expected to stimulate private investment in green technologies, enhancing innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government introduced tax incentives in 2010 for financial instruments that invest in green technology.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform provides a significant incentive for private sector investment in green technologies, which is essential for fostering innovation in this area.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced tax incentives in 2010 for financial instruments that invest in green technology and industry.

reforms.description: The Korean government plans to promote innovation in green technologies by increasing its share in public R&D, focusing on basic research in areas related to large-scale projects by the private sector and in technologies that are not yet commercially viable.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding for green technologies is expected to stimulate innovation and facilitate the transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to promote innovation in green technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing public R&D in green technologies, which is crucial for long-term environmental and economic goals.
reforms.source_quote: Promote innovation in green technologies by increasing its share in public R&D, focusing on basic research.

reforms.description: The Korean government aims to improve the overall innovation framework by increasing spending on basic research and enhancing collaboration between government research institutes, universities, and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration and increasing funding for basic research is expected to lead to more effective innovation outcomes and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to improve the overall innovation framework.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it seeks to strengthen the innovation ecosystem through increased funding and collaboration, which is essential for sustainable growth.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the overall innovation framework by spending more on basic research, closely linking government research institutes, universities and industry.

reforms.description: The Korean government plans to double the share of its basic R&D in green technology to 35% by 2012, aiming to enhance innovation in renewable energy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for basic R&D in green technology is expected to foster innovation and contribute to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government will double the share of its basic R&D in green technology.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D in a critical area for future economic growth and environmental sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The government will double the share of its basic R&D in green technology to 35% by 2012.
